Para indicates purpose: I study in order to learn.
Por indicates movement through/around: We walked through Lisbon.
Por means 'per' in frequency expressions: three times per week.

questions.questionText: 'Preciso disto ___ amanhã.' (I need this by tomorrow)
questions.explanation: Para indicates a deadline: by tomorrow.

questions.questionText: What does 'Fiz isto por ti' mean?
questions.questionTextPt: O que significa 'Fiz isto por ti'?
options: I did this for your sake / because of you
I did this instead of you
questions.correctAnswer: I did this for your sake / because of you
questions.explanation: 'Por ti' = for your sake, on your behalf, because of you. 'Para ti' would mean intended for you as recipient.

questions.questionTextPt: Qual é a contração de 'por + o'?
questions.explanation: Por contracts with articles: por + o = pelo, por + a = pela.

questions.questionText: 'O livro foi escrito ___ ela.' (The book was written by her)
questions.questionTextPt: 'O livro foi escrito ___ ela.' — que preposição?
questions.explanation: In passive voice, the agent uses 'por': written BY her = escrito por ela.

questions.questionText: Quick test: if you can replace 'for' with 'in order to', use:
questions.questionTextPt: Teste rápido: se podes substituir 'for' por 'in order to', usar:
questions.explanation: Para expresses purpose. 'I study FOR learning' = 'I study IN ORDER TO learn' → Estudo para aprender.

questions.questionText: 'Fiz isso ___ amor.' (I did that out of love)
questions.explanation: Por indicates motivation/cause: out of love, because of love.

questions.questionText: 'O presente é ___ a minha mãe.' (The gift is for my mum)
questions.questionTextPt: 'O presente é ___ a minha mãe.' — que preposição?
questions.explanation: Para indicates recipient: the gift is intended for my mum.

questions.questionText: 'Vou ficar ___ duas semanas.' (I'll stay for two weeks)
questions.explanation: Por indicates duration: for (a period of) two weeks.

personal-infinitive.title: Personal Infinitive
personal-infinitive.summary: A uniquely Portuguese feature — the infinitive that conjugates for person. Essential for expressing who does what in complex sentences.
personal-infinitive.intro:
The personal infinitive is one of the few grammar features unique to Portuguese. Unlike Spanish, French, or Italian, Portuguese can conjugate the infinitive to show who the subject is. It's used after prepositions, in subordinate clauses, and when the subject of the infinitive is different from the main verb.

The endings are simple: eu (nothing), tu (-es), ele/ela (nothing), nós (-mos), eles/elas (-em). So for 'falar': falar, falares, falar, falarmos, falarem. The hardest part isn't the formation — it's knowing WHEN to use it.

rules.rule: Formation: add endings to the infinitive — eu: ∅, tu: -es, ele: ∅, nós: -mos, eles: -em.
rules.rulePt: Formação: adicionar terminações ao infinitivo — eu: ∅, tu: -es, ele: ∅, nós: -mos, eles: -em.

examples.en: It's important for us to talk about this.

examples.pt: Antes de saíres, fecha a porta.
examples.en: Before you leave, close the door.
It's necessary for them to study more.
I asked for us to take a break.

rules.rule: Use it when the infinitive has a DIFFERENT subject from the main verb.
rules.rulePt: Usar quando o infinitivo tem um sujeito DIFERENTE do verbo principal.

examples.pt: Pedi para eles virem mais cedo.
examples.en: I asked them to come earlier.
It's hard for us to understand.

examples.pt: É melhor vocês ficarem em casa.
examples.en: It's better for you to stay home.

rules.rule: Commonly used after prepositions: para, antes de, depois de, sem, ao, apesar de.
rules.rulePt: Usado frequentemente após preposições: para, antes de, depois de, sem, ao, apesar de.

examples.pt: Sem sabermos a verdade, não podemos decidir.
examples.en: Without knowing the truth, we can't decide.
After eating dinner, we went for a walk.

examples.pt: Ao chegarem a casa, começou a chover.
examples.en: When they arrived home, it started raining.

rules.rule: Same subject = regular infinitive. Different subjects = personal infinitive.
rules.rulePt: Mesmo sujeito = infinitivo regular. Sujeitos diferentes = infinitivo pessoal.

examples.en: I want to talk to you. (regular infinitive)

examples.pt: É melhor falares com ele. (different: tu)
examples.en: It's better for you to talk to him. (personal)

tips: The personal infinitive is one of the hardest things for non-native speakers. Don't worry about perfecting it — even getting it roughly right marks you as an advanced learner.
In spoken EP, people sometimes skip the personal infinitive and use the regular one. You'll still be understood.
The nós (-mos) and eles/elas (-em) forms are the most useful — they clearly show who's doing the action. The eu and ele forms look identical to the regular infinitive.
tipsPt: O infinitivo pessoal é uma das coisas mais difíceis para falantes não-nativos. Não te preocupes em aperfeiçoá-lo.
Na fala, as pessoas às vezes usam o infinitivo regular em vez do pessoal.
As formas nós (-mos) e eles (-em) são as mais úteis.

questions.questionText: What are the personal infinitive endings?
questions.questionTextPt: Quais são as terminações do infinitivo pessoal?
questions.explanation: Personal infinitive endings: eu (nothing), tu -es, ele (nothing), nós -mos, eles -em.

questions.questionText: When should you use the personal infinitive?
questions.questionTextPt: Quando se deve usar o infinitivo pessoal?
options: When the infinitive has a different subject from the main verb
questions.correctAnswer: When the infinitive has a different subject from the main verb
questions.explanation: Use the personal infinitive when the subject of the infinitive differs from the main verb's subject.

questions.questionText: 'É importante _____ sobre isso.' (for us to talk)
questions.explanation: We (nós) are the subject → falarmos (personal infinitive with -mos).

questions.questionText: 'Antes de _____, fecha a porta.' (before you leave)
questions.questionTextPt: 'Antes de _____, fecha a porta.' (antes de tu saíres)
questions.explanation: Tu is the subject → saíres (personal infinitive with -es).

questions.questionText: Which sentence uses the personal infinitive correctly?
questions.questionTextPt: Que frase usa o infinitivo pessoal corretamente?
options: Pedi para eles virem mais cedo.
questions.correctAnswer: Pedi para eles virem mais cedo.
questions.explanation: Different subjects: eu (asked) + eles (to come) → virem. The others incorrectly use personal infinitive when subjects are the same.

questions.questionText: 'Sem _____ a verdade, não podemos decidir.' (without us knowing)
questions.explanation: Nós is the implicit subject → sabermos.

questions.questionText: 'Depois de _____, fomos passear.' (after we had dinner)
questions.explanation: Nós is the subject of both clauses, but after 'depois de' the personal infinitive clarifies: jantarmos.

questions.questionText: Is the personal infinitive unique to Portuguese?
questions.questionTextPt: O infinitivo pessoal é exclusivo do português?
options: No, all Romance languages have it
questions.explanation: The personal (inflected) infinitive is a distinctive feature of Portuguese — no other major Romance language has it.

questions.questionText: 'Ao _____ a casa, começou a chover.' (when they arrived)
questions.explanation: Eles is the subject → chegarem (personal infinitive with -em).

questions.questionText: Why doesn't 'Quero falar contigo' need the personal infinitive?
questions.questionTextPt: Porque é que 'Quero falar contigo' não precisa do infinitivo pessoal?
options: Because the subject is the same (eu) for both verbs
questions.correctAnswer: Because the subject is the same (eu) for both verbs
questions.explanation: Same subject (eu quero + eu falar) → regular infinitive. Personal infinitive is for different subjects.

questions.questionText: 'É melhor _____ em casa.' (for you all to stay)
questions.explanation: Vocês/eles is the subject → ficarem.

questions.questionText: Which preposition does NOT commonly precede the personal infinitive?
questions.questionTextPt: Que preposição NÃO precede habitualmente o infinitivo pessoal?
questions.explanation: Para, sem, antes de, depois de, ao, apesar de commonly precede it. 'Com' is not typical.
Eu form of the personal infinitive is identical to the regular infinitive: saber (eu saber = saber).

questions.questionText: Which forms of the personal infinitive look identical to the regular infinitive?
questions.questionTextPt: Que formas do infinitivo pessoal são iguais ao infinitivo regular?
questions.explanation: Eu and ele/ela have no added ending, so they look the same as the regular infinitive.
Nós is the subject → estarmos (personal infinitive of estar).

passive-voice.title: Passive Voice
passive-voice.summary: How to say 'the book was written by...' — shifting focus from who did it to what was done.
passive-voice.intro:
The passive voice shifts focus from who performs the action to what receives it. Instead of 'Saramago wrote the book' (active), you say 'The book was written by Saramago' (passive). Portuguese has two ways to form the passive: the 'ser' passive (formal, clear) and the 'se' passive (more natural in everyday EP).

The 'se' passive is everywhere in Portugal — on signs ('Vendem-se apartamentos'), in newspapers, and in conversation. It's often more natural than the 'ser' passive, which can sound formal or translated from English.

rules.rule: Ser passive: ser (conjugated) + past participle (agrees in gender/number). Agent introduced with 'por'.
rules.rulePt: Passiva com ser: ser (conjugado) + particípio passado (concorda em género/número). Agente com 'por'.

examples.pt: O livro foi escrito por Saramago.
examples.en: The book was written by Saramago.

examples.pt: Os alunos foram avaliados pelo professor.
examples.en: The students were evaluated by the teacher.

rules.rule: The passive works in any tense by changing 'ser': present, preterite, imperfect, future, etc.
rules.rulePt: A passiva funciona em qualquer tempo, alterando 'ser': presente, pretérito, imperfeito, futuro, etc.

examples.pt: O jantar é preparado pela minha mãe.
examples.en: Dinner is prepared by my mother. (present)

examples.pt: O ladrão foi apanhado pela polícia.
examples.en: The thief was caught by the police. (preterite)

examples.pt: As aulas eram dadas em português.
examples.en: The classes were given in Portuguese. (imperfect)

examples.pt: O projeto será concluído em maio.
examples.en: The project will be completed in May. (future)

rules.rule: Se passive: verb + se. Much more common in everyday EP. The verb agrees with the subject.
rules.rulePt: Passiva com 'se': verbo + se. Muito mais comum no PE quotidiano.

examples.en: Portuguese is spoken. (lit: one speaks Portuguese)
Apartments for sale. (lit: apartments sell themselves)
You eat well here. / The food is good here.

tips: The 'se' passive is MUCH more common in everyday EP than 'ser + past participle'. Prioritise learning the 'se' construction.
Past participles must agree in gender and number with the subject: escrito/escrita/escritos/escritas.
Common irregular past participles: fazer → feito, dizer → dito, ver → visto, abrir → aberto, escrever → escrito, pôr → posto.
On signs and ads, the 'se' passive is standard: 'Procura-se empregado' (Employee wanted), 'Aceita-se cartão' (Cards accepted).
tipsPt: A passiva com 'se' é muito mais comum no PE do que a passiva com 'ser'.
Os particípios passados concordam em género e número com o sujeito.
Em anúncios, a passiva com 'se' é padrão: 'Vendem-se apartamentos'.

questions.questionText: How do you say 'The book was written by Saramago'?
questions.questionTextPt: Como se diz 'The book was written by Saramago'?
options: O livro foi escrito por Saramago.
O livro está escrito por Saramago.
questions.correctAnswer: O livro foi escrito por Saramago.
questions.explanation: Ser passive: ser (preterite: foi) + past participle (escrito) + por + agent.
Se passive: Vendem-se = are sold/for sale. The verb agrees with apartamentos (plural).

questions.questionText: Which passive construction is more common in everyday EP?
questions.questionTextPt: Qual construção passiva é mais comum no PE do dia a dia?
questions.explanation: The se passive is much more natural in everyday EP: 'Fala-se português', 'Come-se bem aqui'.
Preterite passive: foi construída (was built — completed past action).

questions.questionText: What is the past participle of 'fazer'?
questions.questionTextPt: Qual é o particípio passado de 'fazer'?
questions.explanation: Fazer has an irregular past participle: feito (done/made).
The participle agrees with 'as cartas' (feminine plural): enviadas.

questions.questionText: How do you say 'Portuguese is spoken here' using the se passive?
questions.questionTextPt: Como se diz 'Portuguese is spoken here' com passiva de 'se'?
questions.explanation: Se passive with EP word order: Aqui fala-se português.

questions.questionText: 'O jantar ___ preparado pela minha mãe.' (is — habitual)
questions.explanation: Present passive for habitual action: é preparado (is prepared).

questions.questionText: What word introduces the agent in the ser passive?
questions.questionTextPt: Que palavra introduz o agente na passiva com 'ser'?
questions.explanation: The agent in the passive is introduced by 'por': escrito POR Saramago.
Singular: aluga-se (one room). If plural: alugam-se quartos.

questions.questionText: What is the past participle of 'ver'?
questions.questionTextPt: Qual é o particípio passado de 'ver'?
questions.explanation: Ver has an irregular past participle: visto (seen).
Future passive: será concluído (will be completed).

questions.questionText: How would you see 'Room for rent' on a sign in Portugal?
questions.questionTextPt: Como se vê 'Room for rent' num anúncio em Portugal?
questions.explanation: Signs in Portugal use the se passive: Aluga-se quarto.

questions.questionText: 'As aulas ___ dadas em português.' (were — habitual past)
questions.explanation: Imperfect passive for habitual past: eram dadas (used to be given).

questions.questionText: What is the past participle of 'abrir'?
questions.questionTextPt: Qual é o particípio passado de 'abrir'?
questions.explanation: Abrir has an irregular past participle: aberto (opened).

reported-speech.title: Reported Speech
reported-speech.summary: How to report what someone said — 'She said that...' 'He told me that...' — and the tense shifts involved.
reported-speech.intro:
Reported speech (discurso indireto) is how you relay what someone else said without quoting them directly. Instead of 'He said: "I'm tired"' (direct speech), you say 'He said that he was tired' (reported speech).

The key challenge is tense shifting: when the reporting verb is in the past (disse, contou), the tenses in the reported clause shift back. Present becomes imperfect, preterite becomes pluperfect, and future becomes conditional.

rules.rule: Basic structure: dizer/contar/perguntar + que + shifted tense.
rules.rulePt: Estrutura básica: dizer/contar/perguntar + que + tempo ajustado.

examples.pt: "Estou cansado." → Ele disse que estava cansado.
examples.en: "I'm tired." → He said he was tired.

examples.pt: "Vou ao Porto." → Ela disse que ia ao Porto.
examples.en: "I'm going to Porto." → She said she was going to Porto.

examples.pt: "Gosto de café." → Disse que gostava de café.
examples.en: "I like coffee." → He said he liked coffee.

rules.rule: Tense shifts: Present → Imperfect. Preterite → Pluperfect. Future/ir+inf → Conditional/ia+inf.
rules.rulePt: Mudanças de tempo: Presente → Imperfeito. Pretérito → Mais-que-perfeito. Futuro → Condicional.

examples.pt: "Comprei um carro." → Disse que tinha comprado um carro.

rules.rule: Time/place shifts: hoje → naquele dia, amanhã → no dia seguinte, aqui → ali/lá, ontem → no dia anterior.
rules.rulePt: Mudanças de tempo/lugar: hoje → naquele dia, amanhã → no dia seguinte, aqui → ali, ontem → no dia anterior.

examples.pt: "Venho amanhã." → Disse que vinha no dia seguinte.
examples.en: "I'll come tomorrow." → He said he was coming the next day.
"I'm here." → He said he was there.

examples.pt: "Fiz isso ontem." → Disse que tinha feito isso no dia anterior.
examples.en: "I did that yesterday." → He said he had done that the day before.

rules.rule: Reported questions: perguntar + se (yes/no) or perguntar + question word (quem, onde, quando, como).
rules.rulePt: Perguntas indiretas: perguntar + se (sim/não) ou perguntar + palavra interrogativa.

examples.pt: "Estás bem?" → Perguntou se eu estava bem.
examples.en: "Are you OK?" → He asked if I was OK.
"Where do you live?" → He asked where I lived.

examples.pt: "Quem fez isso?" → Perguntou quem tinha feito isso.
examples.en: "Who did that?" → He asked who had done that.

tips: In casual spoken EP, people often don't shift tenses fully. 'Ele disse que está cansado' (present kept) is common, even though 'estava' is technically correct.
The word 'que' is almost always required after dizer/contar. Don't drop it like English sometimes drops 'that'.
For reported questions, never use a question mark — it's a statement about what was asked, not a question itself.
tipsPt: Na fala informal, as pessoas muitas vezes não fazem todas as mudanças de tempo.
A palavra 'que' é quase sempre necessária depois de dizer/contar.
Nas perguntas indiretas, nunca se usa ponto de interrogação.

questions.questionText: '"Estou cansado."' → How do you report this?
questions.explanation: Present shifts to imperfect in reported speech: está → estava.

questions.questionText: What tense does the present become in reported speech?
questions.questionTextPt: Em que tempo se transforma o presente no discurso indireto?
questions.explanation: Present → Imperfect: 'Estou' → 'Disse que estava'.

questions.questionText: '"Comprei um carro."' → How do you report this?
options: Disse que tinha comprado um carro.
questions.correctAnswer: Disse que tinha comprado um carro.
questions.explanation: Preterite shifts to pluperfect: comprei → tinha comprado.

questions.questionText: How do you report a yes/no question?
questions.questionTextPt: Como se relata uma pergunta de sim/não?
questions.explanation: 'Estás bem?' → Perguntou SE eu estava bem.

questions.questionText: '"Vou viajar."' → Report using past reporting verb:
questions.explanation: Ir + infinitive shifts to ia + infinitive: vou → ia.
Tomorrow → the next day: amanhã → no dia seguinte.

questions.questionText: '"Onde moras?"' → How do you report this?
questions.explanation: Reported question with question word: perguntou onde + shifted tense. No question mark.

questions.questionText: Should you use a question mark in reported questions?
questions.questionTextPt: Deve-se usar ponto de interrogação nas perguntas indiretas?
questions.explanation: Reported questions are statements about what was asked — no question mark.

questions.questionText: What does the future tense become in reported speech?
questions.questionTextPt: Em que se transforma o futuro no discurso indireto?
questions.explanation: Future → Conditional: 'Farei' → 'Disse que faria'.
Present → Imperfect: gosto → gostava.

questions.questionText: Can you drop 'que' after 'disse' in Portuguese?
questions.explanation: Unlike English (where 'that' is often optional), Portuguese almost always keeps 'que'.
Here → there: aqui → ali or lá.
Yes/no question → perguntar + se + shifted tense. No question mark.

options: Disse que faria isso no dia seguinte.
questions.correctAnswer: Disse que faria isso no dia seguinte.
questions.explanation: Future → Conditional + time shift: farei → faria, amanhã → no dia seguinte.

questions.questionText: In casual EP speech, do people always shift tenses in reported speech?
questions.questionTextPt: No PE informal, as pessoas fazem sempre as mudanças de tempo?
options: No, they often keep the original tense
questions.correctAnswer: No, they often keep the original tense
questions.explanation: In casual speech, 'Ele disse que está cansado' (present kept) is common and accepted.

pluperfect.title: Pluperfect (Past Perfect)
pluperfect.summary: Expressing what had happened before something else — 'I had already eaten when she arrived.'
pluperfect.intro:
The pluperfect (mais-que-perfeito) expresses an action that happened BEFORE another past action: 'I had already eaten when she arrived.' In Portuguese, there are two forms: the compound pluperfect (tinha + past participle), which everyone uses in speech, and the simple pluperfect (-ra endings), which is literary and formal.

Focus on the compound form — it's what real people say. The simple form is worth recognising when you see it in writing, but you'll rarely need to produce it.

rules.rule: Compound pluperfect (common): ter in the imperfect (tinha, tinhas, tinha, tínhamos, tinham) + past participle.
rules.rulePt: Mais-que-perfeito composto (comum): ter no imperfeito + particípio passado.

examples.pt: Eu tinha comido antes de ela chegar.
examples.en: I had eaten before she arrived.
They had left when I called.
We had already finished the work.

examples.pt: Ela nunca tinha visto o mar.
examples.en: She had never seen the sea.

rules.rule: Simple pluperfect (literary/formal): single verb form with -ra endings. Rare in modern speech.
rules.rulePt: Mais-que-perfeito simples (literário/formal): forma verbal com terminações -ra. Raro na fala moderna.

examples.pt: Speech: Ele tinha feito tudo sozinho.
examples.en: He had done everything alone. (what people actually say)
He had never seen such a thing.

rules.rule: Irregular past participles used with the pluperfect: fazer→feito, dizer→dito, ver→visto, abrir→aberto, escrever→escrito, pôr→posto, vir→vindo.
rules.rulePt: Particípios passados irregulares: fazer→feito, dizer→dito, ver→visto, abrir→aberto, escrever→escrito, pôr→posto.

examples.en: I had never seen anything like that.

examples.pt: Ela já tinha escrito a carta.
examples.en: She had already written the letter.

examples.pt: Tinham posto a mesa quando cheguei.
examples.en: They had set the table when I arrived.

examples.pt: Ele tinha dito a verdade desde o início.
examples.en: He had told the truth from the beginning.

tips: Use the compound form (tinha + participle) in conversation. The simple form (-ra) sounds archaic in modern EP.
The simple pluperfect is sometimes used in place of the conditional in EP: 'Eu quisera ir' = 'I would have liked to go.' You'll see this in older texts.
Think of the pluperfect as 'the past of the past' — it's one step further back in time than the preterite.
tipsPt: Usa a forma composta (tinha + particípio) na conversa. A forma simples (-ra) soa arcaica.
O mais-que-perfeito simples é às vezes usado em vez do condicional em textos formais.
Pensa no mais-que-perfeito como 'o passado do passado'.

questions.questionText: How do you say 'I had eaten' in EP (spoken form)?
questions.questionTextPt: Como se diz 'I had eaten' em PE (forma falada)?
questions.explanation: Compound pluperfect: tinha + past participle → tinha comido.

questions.questionText: Which form of the pluperfect do people actually use in speech?
questions.questionTextPt: Qual forma do mais-que-perfeito se usa na fala?
questions.explanation: The compound form is standard in modern speech. The simple form is literary/formal.
Past before another past → pluperfect: tinham saído (they had left).

questions.questionText: What is the past participle of 'fazer'?
questions.questionTextPt: Qual é o particípio passado de 'fazer'?
questions.explanation: Fazer has an irregular past participle: feito.
Pluperfect: tinha + visto (irregular past participle of ver).

questions.questionText: What tense is 'tinha' in the compound pluperfect?
questions.questionTextPt: Em que tempo está 'tinha' no mais-que-perfeito composto?
questions.explanation: The compound pluperfect uses ter in the imperfect: tinha, tinhas, tinha, tínhamos, tinham.
Nós form of ter in the imperfect: tínhamos.

questions.questionText: What is the past participle of 'escrever'?
questions.questionTextPt: Qual é o particípio passado de 'escrever'?
questions.explanation: Escrever has an irregular past participle: escrito.

questions.questionText: 'Ele fizera tudo sozinho' — what register is this?
questions.questionTextPt: 'Ele fizera tudo sozinho' — que registo é este?
questions.explanation: The simple pluperfect (-ra form) is literary/formal. In speech: 'Ele tinha feito tudo sozinho.'
Pôr has an irregular past participle: posto.

questions.questionText: The pluperfect expresses an action that happened:
questions.questionTextPt: O mais-que-perfeito exprime uma ação que aconteceu:
options: At the same time as another past action
questions.explanation: The pluperfect is 'the past of the past' — something that had happened before another past event.
Compound pluperfect: tinha + escrito (irregular past participle of escrever).
